commit.message:
Claude/setup releases ci cd r6x9 d (#2)

* feat: add CI/CD pipelines, release workflow, and Arch Linux packaging

- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (clippy + svelte-check on PRs)
- Add release workflow triggered by version tags (builds Linux + Windows,
  creates GitHub Release with .deb, .rpm, .AppImage, .msi, .exe)
- Extend tauri.conf.json bundle metadata (deb/rpm depends, descriptions)
- Sync package.json version to 0.1.0 to match Cargo.toml
- Add version bump script (scripts/bump-version.sh)
- Add AUR PKGBUILDs: snazzgit-bin (binary) and snazzgit-git (source)
- Add freedesktop .desktop entry for Linux app launchers
- Add CachyOS repo submission guide

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

* fix: resolve clippy warnings that fail CI with -D warnings

- Wrap std::env::set_var in unsafe block (required since Rust 1.83)
- Remove identical if/else branches in create_branch
- Remove dead no-op loop in get_commit_diff
- Eliminate redundant let rebinds in stash functions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

* fix: replace deprecated libappindicator3-dev and fix remaining clippy warnings

- Use libayatana-appindicator3-dev (libappindicator3-dev removed in Ubuntu 24.04)
- Fix collapsible_else_if in graph.rs
- Remove redundant clone of AppHandle in remotes.rs
- Apply same dep fix to release.yml

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

* fix: resolve additional clippy warnings (manual_let_else, unnecessary_map_or)

Warnings fixed:
- theme.rs: `map_or(false, ...)` → `is_some_and(...)` (clippy::unnecessary_map_or)
- theme.rs: match-to-return → `let...else` (clippy::manual_let_else)
- recent.rs: match-to-return → `let...else` (clippy::manual_let_else)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

* fix: resolve clippy manual_flatten and needless_borrows_for_generic_args warnings

Use .flatten() on branch iterators instead of manual if-let-Ok patterns,
and remove unnecessary borrows on reset_default path arguments.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

* fix: add @tauri-apps/cli to devDependencies for CI builds

npx tauri build failed in the release workflow because the Tauri CLI
package was not listed as a dependency.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YW8qh3iaLxnSfR6mGkxMRu

commit.message:
Add LP debounce, component gallery, error retry, useCanvas, prop docs, CI, useSearch, useTags

Feature #1: Live Preview debounce
- Only rebuild decorations when doc changes or cursor crosses a JSX block
- Cursor movement within non-JSX areas no longer triggers rebuilds

Feature #2: Component gallery command
- "Show component gallery" generates a note listing all registered
  components with props parsed from @prop comments and destructuring

Feature #3: Better error recovery
- ErrorBoundary retry increments a key, forcing React to re-create
  the child tree from scratch (not just reset error state)

Feature #4: useCanvas hook
- useCanvas(drawFn, deps) returns a ref for <canvas> elements
- Clears and redraws on dependency changes

Feature #5: Component prop docs
- parsePropsFromSource reads // @prop comments and const {} = props
- Gallery command uses this to show prop tables

Feature #6: GitHub Actions CI
- .github/workflows/ci.yml runs tsc, tests, build on push/PR
- Checks bundle size (warns if >1 MB)

Feature #7: useSearch hook
- useSearch(query, maxResults) does full-text vault search
- Returns {results: [{path, name, matches}], loading}

Feature #8: useTags hook
- useTags(filterTag?) returns all tags with counts
- Filters files by tag when filterTag is provided

62 tests, 14 example pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build complete M-Crypto platform: Django backend + React Native mobile app

Backend (Django 5 + DRF):
- Phone+PIN+OTP auth with bcrypt, Google OAuth, JWT tokens
- Multi-currency wallets (USDT/BTC/ETH/SOL/KES) with double-entry ledger
- Payment saga pattern with 3-layer idempotency prevention
- M-Pesa Daraja API client (STK Push, B2B, B2C, Reversal) with RSA SecurityCredential
- Rate engine: CoinGecko + forex composition with 30s rate locking
- KYC tiered daily limits, device fingerprinting, progressive PIN lockout
- M-Pesa IP whitelist middleware, health check endpoint
- Admin dashboard with transaction management and CSV export
- Production settings: Sentry, WhiteNoise, JSON logging, DB pooling
- Docker Compose with PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, Celery workers
- 35+ tests covering auth, wallets, saga, idempotency, rates

Mobile (React Native + Expo):
- Dark theme fintech design (teal primary, amber accent)
- 4-tab navigation: Home, Pay, Wallet, Profile
- Complete payment flow: PayBill/Till → Rate Lock → PIN Confirm → Success
- Auth flow: Phone → OTP → Name → PIN (register) / Phone → PIN (login)
- Balance card, rate ticker, transaction history, deposit modal
- Error boundary, network status, loading screen, biometric auth
- NativeWind (Tailwind), TanStack Query, expo-router, expo-secure-store
- EAS Build profiles for dev/preview/production

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: explicitly select ai_* columns in GET /api/jobs/[id] + version stamp

The wildcard select('*') relies on PostgREST schema cache which can be
stale — ai_score comes back as undefined (not null) causing empty score
pills instead of '—'. Explicitly naming the ai_* columns bypasses cache
and guarantees they're always returned.

Add v8b version stamp to sidebar so we can confirm new code is running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component hot reload, CSS scoping, URL imports, export HTML, useQuery

Feature #1: Component hot reload
- When a component file in the components folder is modified, the
  active view is re-rendered automatically with a Notice

Feature #2: CSS scoping via <Style> component
- <Style>{".btn { padding: 8px }"}</Style> auto-scopes CSS to the
  component's container using auto-generated class names
- Rules are prefixed with a unique scope class

Feature #4: URL imports via useImport hook
- useImport("https://esm.sh/library") loads ES modules from CDN
- Returns {module, loading, error} with in-memory caching
- Uses dynamic import() via new Function to bypass bundler

Feature #5: Export to static HTML
- Command "Export current note JSX to static HTML" creates a
  copy of the note with JSX source preserved as code blocks

Feature #6: useQuery hook
- Generic async data hook: useQuery(key, fetchFn, {cacheMs})
- Returns {data, loading, error, refetch}
- Optional in-memory cache with TTL
- Abstracts the useState+useEffect+loading+error pattern

4 new tests (59 total), example page 13-advanced-features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(source-postgres): implement PartitionedSource and CdcSource feature traits

Move CDC and partitioned-read dispatch out of Source::read() into
dedicated trait methods. The proc macro now handles routing based on
the manifest-declared features (PartitionedRead, Cdc), so Source::read()
only needs the default non-partitioned path.

commit.message:
feat: #11 kubernetes log streaming and runtime auto-detection (#19)

* feat: #11 kubernetes log streaming and runtime auto-detection

- Add KubernetesSource implementing LogSource via k8s.io/client-go
  - Lists + watches pods in current namespace using List/Watch pattern
  - Streams logs from all running containers in each non-self pod
  - Source format: "podName/containerName" for clear log attribution
  - Watch error recovery: exponential backoff (1s→30s) + resourceVersion
    tracking; 410 Gone triggers full re-list before resuming watch
  - Self-exclusion via exact HOSTNAME == pod name match (downward API)
  - Falls back to local kubeconfig for KIND-based development
- Add DetectRuntime() in internal/runtime/detect.go
  - Priority: STRIXLOG_ENV override → K8s token probe → Docker socket probe → fail fast
- Update main.go to call DetectRuntime() instead of hardcoding DockerSource
- Unit tests with fake client and test hook; integration tests in separate ticket

* chore: [no-issue] add Go testing patterns and k8s fake client notes to LEARNING.md

* fix: #11 address PR review comments

- detect_test.go: remove useless no-op test; keep only fileExists tests
- source.go: fix TOCTOU by moving exists-check into startContainerStreamer
  (atomic check+insert under single lock; cancel context if already running)
- source.go: replace tailLines temp var with ptr.To[int64](0) and add comment
  explaining why nil cannot be used (nil = all history, 0 = no history)
- source_test.go: remove time.Sleep from synchronous negative paths (no
  goroutines are launched on skip paths so checks are immediate); replace
  async watch sleep with barrier sentinel event

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(types): rename Feature::BulkLoadCopy to Feature::BulkLoad

Broaden the variant name to cover any bulk-load mechanism (COPY, multipart
upload, load jobs, etc.) rather than tying it to the PostgreSQL COPY protocol.
A serde alias preserves backward compatibility with existing manifests that
use the old "bulk_load_copy" serialized form.

using Claude Haiku instead of cycling through a static pool. Each question is
tailored to avoid repeats within the same room session.

Flow: Try LLM (8s timeout) → parse JSON response → validate → fallback to
static pool if LLM fails or is unavailable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add description prefix to Amazon link

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace const destructuring with with() for scope injection

const destructuring only includes keys present at build time — if
components aren't registered yet, they're undeclared (not just
undefined). with(__scope__) makes ALL scope properties available
as variables via the scope chain, resolving at access time.

This permanently fixes the component library timing issue: components
registered at any point (before or after evaluation) are accessible
because the scope object's dynamic getters are checked on each access.

new Function() creates sloppy-mode functions where with() is allowed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add feature contract enforcement design

Three-tier enforcement for Feature declarations:
1. SDK trait contracts (compile-time) — feature traits enforced by proc macro
2. Conformance test harness (test-time) — generic harness in rapidbyte-sdk
3. Host-side runtime sanity checks — lightweight post-execution warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: split connect/read timeout; larger connection pool

- _timeout stored as (connect, read) tuple: connect capped at
  min(timeout, 10)s so unreachable hosts fail fast; read uses the
  full configured timeout. Transparent to callers — timeout parameter
  unchanged.
- HTTPAdapter always mounted (even retries=0) with pool_connections=4
  and pool_maxsize=20, improving throughput for concurrent callers
  using ThreadPoolExecutor.

commit.message:
Fix component library not loaded before code blocks render

ComponentLoader.loadAll() was deferred to onLayoutReady, which fires
AFTER views are rendered. Code block processors ran first, so file-based
components (Card, StatusBadge, etc.) weren't in scope yet.

Fix: load persistent state and component library eagerly in onload(),
before registering processors. Components are now guaranteed to be in
the registry when the first code block renders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Lower deployment target to macOS 15, fix Info.plist per PR review

- Set deployment target to macOS 15.0 (Sequoia) for broader compatibility
- Use $(MARKETING_VERSION) and $(CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION) build settings
  instead of hardcoded version strings in Info.plist
- Remove duplicate UTI and document type definitions from Info.plist,
  keeping project.yml as the single source of truth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: battle room TenantID empty — bot messages silently dropped by broadcastToRoom

The match service doesn't populate tenant_id for matches, causing all battle rooms
to have TenantID="". Since broadcastToRoom returns early when TenantID is empty,
ALL bot messages (engagement bots, quiz bot) were silently dropped.

Fix: default TenantID to "default" (matching client JWT default) in both
InitBattleRooms and pollBattleRooms. Also adds admin quiz trigger endpoint
(POST /api/v1/admin/quiz/trigger) for on-demand testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix championship feature data leakage in feature engineering

championship_points used cumsum() without shift, so each race row
included its own points — leaking the result into the prediction.
Now uses cumsum().shift(1).fillna(0) so each row only sees standings
from before that race. This was causing inflated pre-weekend win
probabilities (e.g. Russell 53% → 13% for 2026 R1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 5 vault-aware hooks: useFrontmatter, useTheme, usePersistentState, useNote, useDataview

New hooks in scope:
- useFrontmatter() — returns current note's YAML frontmatter, reactive to changes
- useTheme() — returns "light" or "dark", updates on Obsidian theme toggle
- usePersistentState(key, default) — like useSharedState but survives note switches
  and restarts (backed by plugin.saveData with debounced writes)
- useNote(path) — read another note: {content, frontmatter, loading, error}
- useDataview(query) — run DQL query: {values, headers, loading, error}

6 new tests (55 total), example page 12-vault-hooks with combined dashboard.

commit.message:
chore: [no-issue] scope CI triggers to main branch to eliminate duplicate runs (#20)

Running on both push and pull_request caused every PR commit to trigger
two identical unit test runs. Scoping both triggers to main means CI fires
once per PR event and once on merge, with no duplication on feature branch pushes.
The integration job condition was redundant after this change and is removed.

commit.message:
Add error source highlighting, JSX insert commands, dev hot-reload notice

Feature #4: Better error display
- renderError now shows source code with the error line highlighted
- Parses line/column from error messages (e.g., "(3:5)")
- Shows 5-line context window around the error line
- Red highlight bar on the error line, line numbers in gutter

Feature #5: JSX insert commands (command palette)
- "Insert JSX block" — basic template with useState
- "Insert JSX component definition" — jsx:component:MyComponent template
- "Insert JSX block with useState + useEffect" — async data loading pattern
- Also added Notice on cache clear command

Feature #6: Dev hot-reload notification
- esbuild watcher writes .hotreload timestamp file on each deploy
- Plugin polls .hotreload every 2s via vault adapter
- Shows Obsidian Notice when a new build is detected:
  "React Renderer rebuilt — reload plugin or restart Obsidian to apply"

fix: bypass CloudFront for Python ingestion download URLs

getSignedUrl() returns a CloudFront URL when CF env vars are set.
The CloudFront distribution may not have a behavior for broll paths
(users/*/media/*/broll/*), causing 403 when Python tries to download.

Use promptDownload:true to force direct S3 presigned URL for the
internal Python service download — no need for CDN in server-to-server.
